steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the vitamin C content of brussels sprouts and apples. The correct answer must explicitly compare both foods on that specific nutrient.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — presents data without comparison: States a fact about one food but does not compare it to the other. ✗
  • Choice B — 'brussels sprouts and apples both contain vitamin C, but brussels sprouts contain more': Directly compares both foods on vitamin C content. ✓
  • Choice C — classifies by food type: Groups foods into categories rather than comparing their vitamin C levels. ✗
  • Choice D — discusses vitamin C sourcing': Addresses where vitamin C comes from, not how the two foods compare. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
